name: Monstera obliqua 'Amazonas B Form'
aboutText: The Monstera obliqua 'Amazonas B Form' is a highly distinct and sought-after cultivar originating from the Amazon basin. Known for its delicate hemiepiphytic growth, this form is characterized by more entire, less perforated foliage compared to other obliqua types, resulting in a unique, continuous lamina. It thrives in the filtered light and high-humidity environments typical of lowland to lower montane rainforests. Its refined, slender aesthetic and slow-growing nature make it a prized specimen for dedicated aroid collectors.

fieldNotes.title: From Deep in the Amazon Basin
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: An Amazonas locality form of Monstera obliqua distinguished from the primary Amazonas form by differences in fenestration pattern and morphological detail — hence the B designation. True Monstera obliqua, from any locality, is characterised by the extremely high fenestration ratio that makes adult leaves appear more hole than blade. If what you have propagates vigorously and fenestrates modestly, it is adansonii. If it grows slowly, fenestrates dramatically, and generally tests your patience, it may well be obliqua. At ultra rare pricing for what is genuinely ultra rare material.
